danger_signals.visual_form: In-game overlay notifications and advisor alerts
danger_signals.meaning: City health declining; citizens suffering; requires medical infrastructure response

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy unit sprites approaching city; visual distinction of enemy soldiers on isometric map
danger_signals.meaning: Foreign armies present; walls and defensive structures needed; potential combat imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual effects on map tiles (flooding, fire, sandstorms); destruction of structures
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental catastrophe; loss of buildings and citizens; requires disaster preparation or response

danger_signals.visual_form: Housing downgrades in visual appearance; advisor warnings; overlay highlighting food shortage
danger_signals.meaning: Famine risk; citizens starving; agricultural or trade systems failing

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual downgrade of residential building sprites from improved to basic appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Unmet citizen demands; services or goods lacking; population dissatisfaction

safety_signals.visual_form: Residential building sprites improve in visual quality and ornamentation
safety_signals.meaning: Citizen demands met; city prosperity increasing; good governance evident

safety_signals.visual_form: Large monument structures appear and visually grow on map; distinctive Egyptian architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Kingdom approval rising; cultural achievement; player meeting Pharaoh's mandates

safety_signals.visual_form: Trade route visual indicators on map; caravan and boat sprites moving between cities
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability; resource flow; financial sustainability

safety_signals.visual_form: Wall structures visible on city perimeter; watchtower sprites placed strategically
safety_signals.meaning: Military readiness; protection against invasion; proactive defense strategy

safety_signals.visual_form: Housing density increases; more citizen sprites visible; populated streets
safety_signals.meaning: City thriving; happiness levels adequate; prosperity increasing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Egyptian architecture style; player-controlled building designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player structures appear as cohesive, ordered settlements
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Maintained farms, irrigation ditches, roads; organized street grid; monument construction sites

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct soldier sprites; different armor and weapon visual designs from player units
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Soldier sprites visually differentiated from player warriors; potentially bearing different cultural markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear on map edges; military encampments; siege positions outside walls

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Named historical Egyptian cities referenced in campaign
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Trade routes on map; caravan and boat sprites; designated trade port areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Palace building; royal decrees and assignments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Palace structure present in city; formal briefing sequences; mission objectives from ruling authority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No enemy unit sprites on map; clear skies; active civilian activity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens freely move through city; construction proceeds without interruption; trade routes open and active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy soldier sprites visible approaching city; walls become highlighted or emphasized; dust/movement effects around armies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens seek shelter or panic; military units mobilize; construction halts or focuses on defenses; trade disrupted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Water inundates floodplain areas; fire spreads across structures; sandstorm visual effects; destroyed building rubble
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens flee affected areas; structures collapse; farms temporarily unusable; recovery period required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Housing upgrades visually; monuments display completion progress with grand visual treatment; markets and industries appear bustling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population increases; more citizen foot traffic; industries operate at capacity; trade flourishes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Housing downgrades visually; markets and warehouses appear depleted; fewer caravan sprites; deteriorated infrastructure appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population stagnates or declines; reduced citizen activity; trade contracts; construction slows or stops

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overlay warnings activated; affected areas marked; hospital buildings emphasized; death animations or grave markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens avoid affected districts; movement restricted; medical staff sprites active; population decline in affected zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monument structure reaches completion with grand visual display; distinctive Egyptian architectural forms fully rendered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Celebration sequences; Kingdom favor boost notification; mission objective completion trigger

environmental_storytelling.example: Campaign missions follow actual Ancient Egyptian historical periods and events; monument types and available goods reflect authentic historical context for each era
Nile River presence indicates fertile floodplain farming potential; absence of certain raw materials on map forces trade dependency and narrative economic pressure
Different monuments assigned in different campaign periods (pyramids in early periods, temples in later) communicates historical progression and technological advancement
Basic housing appearance reflects unsatisfied citizens; progressive upgrades in housing sprite quality communicate improving living standards and met citizen demands
Player's choice of road patterns, farm placement, and wall positioning tells visual story of city planning philosophy and defensive priorities
Campaign briefings introduce historical Egyptian rulers; their visual representation and stated objectives communicate political context and historical authenticity
Visible destruction from flooding or other disasters on map tiles serves as environmental reminder of vulnerability and need for disaster planning

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible movement through streets; housing visually upgrades; increased population density; orderly street traffic
Reduced street activity; housing visually downgrades; population decline; potential unrest or emigration
Industry building sprites show active animation; goods appear in warehouses; caravan sprites depart with exports
Industry buildings show no activity animation; no goods produced; warehouses accumulate only raw materials
Soldier sprites positioned on walls; patrol patterns visible along perimeter; ready stance animation
Enemy soldier sprites move toward city; visible formation; siege positioning outside walls; potential attack animation
Caravan sprites regularly traverse trade routes; boats visible on Nile; goods flow into and out of city warehouses
No caravan movement; routes appear inactive; boats absent from waterways; warehouse supplies decline
Farm plots show crop sprites; harvest activity visible; irrigation ditches active; grain flows to granaries
Farm sprites inundated or destroyed; no harvest activity; fields appear barren; granaries empty

notes: Pharaoh uses a fixed isometric perspective with rotatable viewing angles (90-degree increments), allowing players to observe city state from multiple angles. The game employs a sophisticated overlay system that can highlight specific issues (food shortage, disease, lack of services) through color-coded or emphasized map regions. Housing visual quality serves as a primary indicator of overall city health and citizen satisfaction—this is a critical manifestation mechanic. The game's adherence to historical Egyptian context (monuments, goods, periods) provides implicit world-state communication through authenticity. Disaster visualization (flooding of Nile delta regions, fire spread, sandstorms) creates environmental peril that directly impacts visible cityscape. The Pharaoh and Kingdom favor system creates a top-down political context that players perceive through mission briefings and objective communications rather than persistent visual world-state indicators. Trade route visibility and caravan/boat sprite activity provide economic health indicators. The campaign's chronological progression through Egyptian historical periods means the available visual set (monuments, building types, unit designs) itself communicates which historical era the player operates within.
